data.title: Metaplanet Increases Bitcoin Holdings With $13.5M Purchase and Bond Issuance
data.description:
• Metaplanet has acquired 162 BTC for $13.5 million at an average price of $83,123, boosting its year-to-date bitcoin yield to 53.2% in 2025.
  • To further expand its bitcoin reserves, the company has issued 2 billion JPY in zero-interest ordinary bonds.

data.title: Metaplanet Increases Bitcoin Holdings With $13.5M Purchase and Bond Issuance
data.description:
• Metaplanet has acquired 162 BTC for $13.5 million at an average price of $83,123, boosting its year-to-date bitcoin yield to 53.2% in 2025.
  • To further expand its bitcoin reserves, the company has issued 2 billion JPY in zero-interest ordinary bonds.

data.title: Franklin Templeton Joins XRP ETF Rush, Files Preliminary Application With SEC
data.description:
• Franklin Templeton has filed for a Franklin XRP ETF, which aims to track the spot price of XRP minus fees, with assets held via Coinbase Custody.
  • The ETF shares will be traded on the Cboe BZX Exchange, and shareholders will not benefit from XRP Ledger forks or airdrops.

data.title: Bitcoin Sees Relief Run to $82K; SEC Delays XRP, DOGE, LTC ETF Filings
data.description:
• Bitcoin recovered to nearly $82,000 after a dip below $78,000, causing a slight increase in major tokens such as Ether, BNB Chain’s bnb, XRP, and Cardano’s ADA.
  • The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has delayed decisions on XRP, Dogecoin, and Litecoin filings, keeping the market on edge.
  • Senator Cynthia Lummis reintroduced the BITCOIN Act, advocating for the U.S. to acquire 1 million BTC as a strategic reserve, sparking speculation about the possible inclusion of major tokens in such a reserve in the future.

data.title: Bitcoin CME Futures Spread Slides to $490, Undoing The 'Trump Bump' in BTC
data.description:
• The bullish sentiment in the bitcoin market following Donald Trump's election victory has fizzled out, as indicated by the narrowing spread between next month and front-month BTC futures on the CME.
  • The market has likely moved past the narrative that a pro-crypto President is beneficial for the industry, with macro correlations now driving the market.
  • The CME futures curve is still in contango.

data.title: Crypto Payments Firm Mesh Raises $82M as Stablecoin Adoption Soars
data.description:
• Crypto payments firm Mesh has raised $82 million in a series B round to expand its global stablecoin-based payments settlement network.
  • The capital was mostly raised in PayPal's PYUSD stablecoin.
  • The firm is building a network that lets users pay with crypto assets, while merchants settle the transaction in stablecoins.

data.title: Ukraine Ceasefire Breakthrough Sends Markets Into Green; Bitcoin Retakes $83K
data.description:
• Ukraine has agreed to a U.S. proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in its war with Russia.
  • The pact is contingent on Russian acceptance.
  • U.S. stock markets moved from sizable losses to gains following the news and crypto turned decidedly higher.

data.title: Bahrain-Regulated Crypto Exchange Enters $1B Tokenized Gold Market as RWA Demand Grows
data.description:
• ATME, a Bahrain-based digital assets exchange, has issued its first tokenized real-world asset starting with gold-backed tokens.
  • Each token represents one kilogram of gold, and can be traded on ATME's secondary market or redeemed for physical gold.
  • The market of tokenized gold is worth around $1.2 billion, with ATME planning to expand its offerings to include other asset classes.

data.title: Robinhood Crypto Trading Fell 29% as February Price Slide Distanced Retail Investors
data.description:
• Robinhood’s crypto trading volume dropped 29% in February, outpacing declines in equities and options trading.
  • Despite the monthly drop, the crypto volumes were 122% higher than in February 2023.
  • The decline coincides with a broader crypto market downturn, with bitcoin and the CoinDesk 20 Index experiencing significant losses.

data.title: Mt. Gox Moves Another $930M Bitcoin as Payout Deadline Looms
data.description:
• Mt. Gox, the defunct crypto exchange, moved some $930 million in bitcoin (BTC) to new wallets, potentially signaling a resumption of creditors payout.
  • The latest transfer follows last week's $1 billion internal reshuffling of assets and a $15 million transfer to crypto custodian and payout partner BitGo.
  • The recent movements could indicate the estate preparing to repay the rest of the assets to users before the October 31 deadline, reigniting concerns of selling pressure in the midst of a crypto market correction.
